breaking_news.title: TeamPCP Executes Massive Trivy Supply Chain Attack Compromising Docker and GitHub
breaking_news.summary:
The TeamPCP threat group successfully compromised the Aqua Security Trivy vulnerability scanner, injecting infostealer malware into official Docker images and GitHub repositories. Attackers hijacked the aquasec-com GitHub organization, defacing 44 repositories and pushing malicious tags to distribute credential-stealing payloads across CI/CD pipelines.

This supply chain breach threatens thousands of developers relying on Trivy for security scanning, potentially exposing sensitive pipeline secrets and authentication credentials. The incident highlights the critical risk of compromised open-source tools and the need for immediate secret rotation across affected environments.

breaking_news.title: Oracle Releases Emergency Patch for Critical Unauthenticated RCE in Identity Manager
breaking_news.summary:
Oracle issued an out-of-band security update to address CVE-2026-21992, a critical vulnerability allowing unauthenticated remote code execution in Identity Manager. The flaw carries a CVSS score of 9.8 and enables attackers to take full control of affected systems over HTTP without any prior authentication.

Enterprise organizations relying on Oracle Identity Manager face immediate risk of system compromise and data theft if patches are not applied urgently. Security researchers indicate the vulnerability may already be exploited in the wild, necessitating rapid mitigation across global infrastructure.

breaking_news.title: Hackers Actively Exploit CVSS 10.0 Quest KACE Vulnerability in Wild Attacks
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actors are actively exploiting CVE-2025-32975, a maximum-severity flaw in Quest KACE Systems Management Appliance systems exposed to the internet. Arctic Wolf observed malicious activity consistent with this exploitation starting the week of March 9, 2026, targeting unpatched SMA systems.

Educational and enterprise sectors using Quest KACE infrastructure are at high risk of complete system hijacking and lateral movement within their networks. Organizations must immediately verify patch status and isolate exposed appliances to prevent unauthorized access and data exfiltration.

breaking_news.title: FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Targeting Signal and WhatsApp Users in Phishing Campaign
breaking_news.summary:
The FBI and CISA issued a joint warning that Russian intelligence-linked actors are conducting phishing campaigns to hijack high-value accounts on Signal and WhatsApp. The campaign specifically targets current and former U.S. government officials, military personnel, and journalists to gain access to private communications.

Compromised messaging accounts allow adversaries to conduct espionage, steal sensitive intelligence, and monitor communications of key national security figures. Users of encrypted messaging platforms must remain vigilant against social engineering attempts impersonating support services.

breaking_news.title: WorldLeaks Ransomware Group Breaches Los Angeles Metro Critical Infrastructure
breaking_news.summary:
The WorldLeaks ransomware group launched an attack against the Los Angeles Metro system, forcing the agency to limit access to internal administrative computer systems. The unauthorized activity disrupted station arrival displays and prevented riders from adding funds to TAP cards online or via support.

This incident highlights the vulnerability of public transit infrastructure to cyberattacks, causing operational disruptions for millions of daily commuters. Local authorities are investigating the breach while working to restore full system access and ensure no customer data was compromised.

breaking_news.title: VoidStealer Malware Bypasses Chrome ABE to Steal Passwords and Cookies
breaking_news.summary:
Researchers discovered VoidStealer, a new infostealer that bypasses Chrome’s Application-Bound Encryption (ABE) using a novel debugger-based technique. Unlike previous methods, this bypass requires neither privilege escalation nor code injection, making it significantly stealthier and harder to detect.

The vulnerability undermines a key security control introduced in Chrome 127, exposing sensitive browser data like passwords and cookies to theft. Users and enterprises must monitor for this malware variant and ensure browsers are updated to mitigate the risk of credential harvesting.

breaking_news.title: North Korean Operative Infiltrates US Company as Fake IT Worker Before Detection
breaking_news.summary:
A suspected North Korean-linked operative successfully passed security checks to secure a remote IT role before being identified and terminated within 10 days. Behavioral analytics and threat intelligence correlation flagged geolocation anomalies and unmanaged device access that revealed the threat.

This incident underscores the evolving threat of state-sponsored actors using insider access to fund national weapons programs and steal intellectual property. Organizations must enhance hiring vetting and continuous monitoring to detect sophisticated identity fraud and insider threats.

breaking_news.title: PolyShell Flaw Exposes 7,500 Magento Sites to Defacement and RCE Attacks
breaking_news.summary:
Sansec disclosed a critical REST API vulnerability in Magento and Adobe Commerce named PolyShell, allowing unauthenticated file uploads and potential remote code execution. Attackers have already defaced over 7,500 sites since late February, uploading executable files across thousands of hostnames.

E-commerce platforms and global brands running vulnerable versions face immediate risk of account takeover and system compromise. Adobe has fixed the issue in a pre-release version, but many stores remain exposed due to lack of backported patches.
